cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The user wants me to figure out the best title for this passage about body language in the office. Let me read through the passage again to make sure I understand the key points. The passage starts by saying body language is very powerful and that it communicates a lot more than words. Then it lists some mistakes to avoid in the office. The tips are about not touching coworkers, maintaining eye contact, having a good handshake, and not leaning the head while listening. Each of these is a specific advice on what not to do with body language in a professional setting. Now, looking at the answer choices: A) Pay attention to your body language at the office. B) How to get along with your co-workers. C) The meaning of body language you should know. D) Different countries have different body language. The passage is specifically about body language in the office and what not to do. So the title should reflect that focus. Option A directly mentions body language in the office, which matches the content. Option B is broader about getting along with coworkers, which isn't the main focus here. The passage is about specific body language tips, not general coworker relations. Option C talks about the meaning of body language in general, but the passage is more about what to avoid in the office. Option D is about cultural differences, which isn't
